mentions.note: Pivoted from plain banking to full corporate/individual bank with lucrative M&A practice (Netflix deal); constantly pairing costs with efficiency; CEO Charlie Sharf visionary; optimistic on quarter and AI tailwinds.

Charitable Trust holding; missed on efficiency ratio (64% vs expected 62.5%); core business sound but stock up 35% in prior 12mo. Sold some to schnitzel (trade around core); still bullish on CEO Charlie Scharf's transformation long-term but stock needs to come down more.
Michelle asks which businesses benefit from AI beyond chips/energy/infra. Banks will benefit most from AI efficiency; Wells Fargo has best handle on it — we own for Charitable Trust.
Winner in software spending rotation; hired technologist from AWS to help bankers find hidden tech savings; benefiting from cost-efficiency narrative.
Long-term turnaround story under CEO Charlie Scharf; committed to stock buybacks; reports Tuesday.
Exposed to private credit holdings; missed quarter; stock trying to bottom; worst of major banks; held in trust only because inexpensive, not conviction.
Bank rally beneficiary on rate environment, strong consumer, deregulation; cheap valuation leaves room for 50%+ rally before reasonably priced.
